content.json:
# Microsoft 365 Activity Baselines — All Queries (v1)

> ⚠️ **Observable telemetry only.** These figures are **not** a measure of productivity, work quality, effort, or hours worked. A higher count does not mean more work was done. Deviations and outliers are **prompts to review**, never proof of underperformance or misconduct. The **primary** model is each user vs their *own* baseline; the organisation views are supporting context. See `docs/assumptions-and-limitations.md`.

Pick a **Date range** (1/7/30/60/90 days or a custom range) and, for the drill-down views, optionally a **User UPN**. Each panel has its own **Description: Show/Hide** toggle. Service accounts, system principals, and B2B guests are excluded throughout.

## Baseline — Model A (user vs own baseline)
**Primary model.** Each user is compared with their *own* previous period of equal length. With a 90-day range selected, the baseline reaches ~180 days back (subject to data retention).

**01 · User vs own baseline (summary).** One row per user comparing this period (**{W_TimeRange:label}**) against that same user's previous equal-length period. Read `ActiveDayChangePct` and `Status` first. Telemetry only — a change is a prompt to ask a question, never a verdict.
**02 · Baseline by workload.** The same own-baseline comparison split per M365 workload, so you can see *where* a pattern shifted (e.g. Teams up, SharePoint down).
**03 · Activity trend.** Daily meaningful-activity trend, one line per workload. Leave the **User UPN** box empty for the whole organisation, or enter a UPN to focus on one person.

**04 · Sign-in active days.** An independent engagement proxy from sign-in logs: distinct days with a successful interactive sign-in, this period vs the user's own baseline. Use it to corroborate the OfficeActivity picture.

**05 · Flagged for review.** The shortlist — only users whose active-day ratio moved outside the ±**{W_NormalBandPct}%** band vs their own baseline, with low-confidence baselines suppressed. "Flagged" = worth a question, not a finding.

## Organisation comparison — Model B (user vs org / peers)
**Supporting context.** Robust comparison (median / MAD / percentiles) against the wider organisation. Not a leaderboard.

**06 · Organisation baseline per workload.** What "normal" looks like across the org per workload — median per-day rate, P25–P90 spread and MAD (robust spread). The reference distribution behind the user-vs-org table.

**07 · User vs organisation.** Each user's per-workload rate placed against the org: percentile band (read first) and a robust modified z-score; `OrgFlag` highlights |z| > **{W_OutlierZ}**. Different roles legitimately differ.
**08 · Organisation trend.** Org-level daily trend per workload as an average **per active user**, so headcount swings don't distort it. The company backdrop for an individual's trend.

**11 · User vs organisation trend.** One user's daily activity overlaid on the org average (per active user) on a single chart — the **gap between the lines** is the signal. Enter a **User UPN** above.

## Drill-down — underlying events & taxonomy
Use these to understand *what* drove a number before drawing any conclusion.

**09 · User event drill-down.** The raw meaningful events behind a user's numbers, newest first. **Enter a User UPN above to populate this table.** Event-level, privacy-sensitive — handle responsibly.

**10 · Workload / operation breakdown.** Every operation seen, with events, distinct users and its taxonomy category + engagement flag. Validates the meaningful-vs-noise taxonomy; any `UNMAPPED` / high-volume ops float to the top.
